hero.sub: Speak naturally in Yoruba, Hausa, Igbo, Pidgin, or English and receive personalised pregnancy, newborn care, nutrition, and vaccination guidance.

features.sub: From pregnancy tracking to newborn care — MamaVoice guides you every step of the way.

voice.title: Voice-First Consultations
voice.desc: Speak naturally in Yoruba, Hausa, Igbo, Pidgin, or English — our AI understands and responds in your language with trusted medical guidance.

specialist.title: Specialist Connect
specialist.desc: Instant access to verified OBGYNs, midwives, and pediatricians across Nigeria for virtual or in-person consultations.

health.title: Health Tracking
health.desc: Monitor blood pressure, weight, baby movements, and key milestones automatically throughout your pregnancy journey.

nutrition.title: Nutrition Guidance
nutrition.desc: Personalized meal plans featuring local Nigerian foods rich in folate, iron, and protein — foods you actually know and love.

vaccine.title: Vaccination Monitor
vaccine.desc: Never miss a prenatal or newborn vaccine. Get smart reminders with a full timeline for mother and baby.

newborn.title: Newborn Care
newborn.desc: Evidence-based guidance for breastfeeding, cord care, diaper rash, fever response, and early childhood milestones.

howItWorks.sub: No complex setup. No data required. Just download, speak, and get the guidance you need.

s1.title: Download & Register
s1.desc: Get MamaVoice on iOS or Android. Register with just your phone number — no email required.

s2.title: Set Your Profile
s2.desc: Enter your due date, language preference, and basic health history. Takes under 2 minutes.

s3.title: Ask by Voice
s3.desc: Tap the mic and speak freely. Ask anything about your pregnancy in your own language.

s4.title: Track Daily Health
s4.desc: Log symptoms, weight, and baby kicks. Get automated reminders for medications and vaccines.

s5.title: Connect with Doctors
s5.desc: Book video or voice consultations with verified specialists from the comfort of your home.

s6.title: Share & Grow
s6.desc: Invite fellow mothers. Share milestones and encouragement in your community group.

voice.sub: MamaVoice understands Yoruba, Hausa, Igbo, Pidgin, and English — responding with trusted medical guidance instantly.
voice.supports: Supports Yoruba · Hausa · Igbo · Pidgin · English
voice.msg1: I am 28 weeks pregnant and my legs are swollen. What should I do?
voice.msg2: Mild swelling during pregnancy is common. Elevate your feet, drink plenty of water, and avoid standing for long periods. If you experience sudden severe swelling or headaches, visit a clinic immediately.
voice.msg3: Thank you. What iron-rich foods can I eat?

dashboard.sub: Track every metric that matters — all beautifully organised in your personal dashboard.
dashboard.tipText: Stay hydrated — drink 8–10 glasses of water daily for optimal fetal development and to ease leg swelling.

nutrition.sub: MamaVoice recommends local Nigerian superfoods packed with nutrients your baby needs to thrive.
nutrition.ctaSub: Get personalised meal plans based on your trimester

moimoi.name: Moi Moi
moimoi.desc: Steamed bean pudding made from blended black-eyed peas — a pregnancy superfood.
moimoi.b1: Rich in folate for neural tube development
moimoi.b2: Iron supports red blood cell production

ugu.name: Ugu Soup
ugu.desc: Fluted pumpkin leaf soup — one of Nigeria's most nutrient-dense vegetables.
ugu.b0: Highest iron content of any Nigerian leaf
ugu.b1: Vitamin A supports fetal eye development
ugu.b2: Calcium and magnesium for bone formation

pap.name: Pap & Groundnut
pap.desc: Fermented corn porridge (akamu) paired with protein-rich groundnuts.
pap.b1: Healthy fats from groundnuts for brain development
pap.b2: High energy — ideal for the third trimester

specialists.sub: Browse trusted nurses, midwives, and doctors. Book virtual consultations right from the app.

webApp.sub: No registration needed. Chat with our AI health assistant instantly. Ask about symptoms, nutrition, vaccinations — anything on your mind.

download.sub: Get personalised health tracking, vaccination reminders, offline voice support, and more — available on Android and iPhone.

footer.newsletterSub: Get maternal health tips and product updates delivered to your inbox.

chat.greetingSub: Your trusted AI health companion. Ask me anything about pregnancy, nutrition, newborn care, or vaccinations.
chat.disclaimer: MamaVoice provides health education, not medical diagnosis. Always consult a doctor for emergencies.
chat.nudge: Get personalised tracking, vaccination reminders & more
chat.p0: I'm 28 weeks pregnant and my legs are swollen. What should I do?
chat.p1: What iron-rich Nigerian foods should I eat in my second trimester?
chat.p2: When is my Tetanus vaccine due during pregnancy?
chat.p3: My newborn won't stop crying at night. Is this normal?
chat.rSwollen: Mild swelling (oedema) is very common in pregnancy, especially in the legs and feet after week 20. Elevate your feet when resting, drink at least 8 glasses of water daily, and avoid standing for long periods. Wear comfortable shoes and sleep on your left side. ⚠️ If you notice sudden severe swelling in your face or hands, or swelling with headaches or blurred vision, please visit a clinic immediately — this could be a sign of preeclampsia.
chat.rIron:
Great question! Iron-rich Nigerian foods you can enjoy safely during pregnancy include:

• Ugu (fluted pumpkin leaves) — one of the richest sources of iron in Nigerian cuisine
• Moi Moi (steamed bean pudding) — packed with iron and protein
• Liver — extremely high in iron (limit to once a week)
• Ofada rice with vegetable stew
• Ogbono soup with leafy greens

Pair these with Vitamin C foods (like oranges or tomatoes) to help your body absorb the iron better.
chat.rVaccine:
The standard vaccination schedule for pregnant women in Nigeria includes:

💉 Tetanus Toxoid 1 (TT1) — given at first antenatal visit
💉 Tetanus Toxoid 2 (TT2) — given 4 weeks after TT1
💉 TT Booster — if you had prior tetanus vaccinations

You may also receive the Influenza vaccine, especially during flu season. Always confirm your schedule with your healthcare provider at every antenatal visit.
chat.rCry:
Newborn crying is completely normal and is their only way to communicate! Common reasons include:

• Hunger (most common — try feeding)
• Dirty diaper
• Needs to be held/comforted
• Overtired or overstimulated
• Gas or tummy discomfort (try gentle tummy massages)

Most babies cry 1–3 hours per day in the first few weeks. If your baby is inconsolable for over 3 hours, has a fever above 38°C, or isn't feeding well, please see a doctor right away.
chat.rPain:
Back pain during pregnancy is very common. Try these safe remedies:

• Gentle prenatal yoga or stretching
• Sleep on your left side with a pillow between your knees
• Wear supportive, flat shoes
• Apply a warm (not hot) compress to the painful area

If the pain is severe or accompanied by fever or urinary symptoms, please consult your doctor.
chat.rNausea:
Morning sickness is very common in the first trimester. Here's what can help:

• Eat small, frequent meals — don't let your stomach get empty
• Try dry crackers or akamu (pap) first thing in the morning
• Sip cold water or ginger tea slowly
• Avoid spicy, fatty, or strongly-smelling foods

If you're vomiting so much you can't keep food or water down, please see a doctor — you may need IV fluids.
chat.rDefault:
That's a great question. MamaVoice is here to support you every step of your pregnancy and newborn journey. For the most accurate guidance on your specific situation, I recommend:

1. Speaking with one of our verified specialists in the app
2. Attending your next antenatal clinic visit
3. Calling your nearest health facility if you have urgent concerns

Is there anything specific about pregnancy, nutrition, vaccinations, or newborn care I can help you with today?
chat.webPreviewBanner: You're using the web preview. Get the full app for the best experience.

chat.langSwitchPrompt: Switching language will clear your current chat. Continue?
chat.emergencyPrompt: If this is urgent, call emergency services immediately
chat.p4: What are danger signs in pregnancy?
chat.howCanIHelp: How can MamaVoice help you today?

privacy.metaDescription: Learn how MamaVoice collects, uses, and protects your personal and health data.
privacy.headline: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At MamaVoice, we are deeply committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you use our maternal and newborn health platform. Please read this policy carefully.
privacy.s1body: We collect information you provide directly when you create an account, use voice consultations, track your health data, or contact us. This includes: your name, phone number, and optional email address; your pregnancy details, due date, and health history; voice recordings used during speech-to-text consultations; health metrics such as blood pressure, weight, baby kicks, and medication schedules; device information including device type, operating system, and browser type; and usage data such as how you interact with MamaVoice features.
privacy.s2body: We use the information we collect to provide, maintain, and improve MamaVoice services; personalise your health guidance and nutrition recommendations based on your pregnancy stage; send you vaccination reminders, medication alerts, and health milestones; process and respond to your voice consultations through our AI system; connect you with verified maternal health specialists when requested; comply with legal obligations and protect the safety of our users; and improve the accuracy and cultural relevance of our AI responses for African mothers.
privacy.s3body: When you use the voice consultation feature, your spoken words are captured by your device's microphone and processed in real-time. Voice data is transmitted securely to our speech recognition service and is not stored permanently on our servers beyond the duration of your session. We use your voice input solely to generate a text transcript for our AI health assistant. Voice recordings are never shared with third parties for advertising purposes. You may disable voice input at any time through your device settings or the MamaVoice app settings.
privacy.s4body: MamaVoice collects and processes health-related information including pregnancy data, symptoms, medical history, and health metrics. This information is treated with the highest level of care and confidentiality. We use this data solely to provide you with personalised health guidance and to improve our services. We do not sell your health information to any third party. Health data is encrypted both in transit and at rest using industry-standard protocols. Access to your health data is strictly limited to authorised MamaVoice personnel on a need-to-know basis.
privacy.s5body: Your personal data is stored on secure servers in Africa and the European Union, in compliance with applicable data protection laws. We implement robust technical and organisational measures including end-to-end encryption (TLS 1.3) for data in transit, AES-256 encryption for data at rest, regular security audits, strict access controls, and automated threat detection. Despite our best efforts, no electronic storage method is 100% secure. If a data breach occurs, we will notify you within 72 hours as required by applicable law.
privacy.s6body: MamaVoice uses artificial intelligence to generate health information and guidance. This information is for educational purposes only and is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Our AI is trained on evidence-based maternal health guidelines but may not always account for your specific medical circumstances. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional before making medical decisions. In an emergency, call 112 or go to your nearest hospital immediately.
privacy.s7body: MamaVoice integrates with third-party services including: Anthropic Claude AI for natural language processing; YarnGPT for African-language text-to-speech; Google Analytics for anonymous usage statistics; and cloud hosting providers for data storage and delivery. These third parties are contractually required to handle your data in accordance with applicable privacy laws and our data processing agreements. We do not sell your personal information to any third party.
privacy.s8body: MamaVoice uses cookies and similar technologies to remember your language preferences and dark mode settings, maintain your session across visits, analyse service interactions to improve user experience, and measure feature effectiveness. We use Google Analytics to collect anonymous usage data. You can disable cookies through your browser settings, though this may affect MamaVoice functionality. We respect the Do Not Track signal where technically feasible.
privacy.s9body: You have the right to: access a copy of the data we hold about you; correct inaccurate or incomplete data; request deletion of your account and all associated data; receive your data in a portable, machine-readable format; object to or restrict the processing of your data; and withdraw consent at any time. To exercise any of these rights, contact us at privacy{'@'}mamavoice.africa. We respond to all requests within 30 days. Deleting your account will permanently remove all your health records, chat history, and personal information.
privacy.s10body: MamaVoice is designed for adults aged 18 and above. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If you believe a child has provided us with personal information without your consent, contact us immediately at privacy{'@'}mamavoice.africa and we will delete that information promptly.
privacy.s11body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make material changes, we will notify you via the MamaVoice app, a notification to your registered phone number, and by updating the 'Last Updated' date above. Your continued use of MamaVoice after any changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.
privacy.s12body: For questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy or your personal data, contact our Privacy Officer: Email: privacy{'@'}mamavoice.africa {'|'} Phone: +234 800 MAMA VOICE {'|'} MamaVoice Technologies Ltd, Lagos, Nigeria. We aim to respond to all privacy enquiries within 5 business days.

terms.metaDescription: Read the Terms of Service governing your use of MamaVoice Companion maternal health platform.
terms.headline: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By accessing or using MamaVoice Companion, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service and our Privacy Policy. Please read these terms carefully before using our service.
terms.s1body: By accessing or using MamaVoice Companion, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service and our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our service. We reserve the right to update these terms at any time, and your continued use of the service constitutes acceptance of any changes.
terms.s2body: MamaVoice is intended for use by adults aged 18 and over who are pregnant, new mothers, or caregivers of newborns. By using this service, you confirm that you are at least 18 years old, you are using the service for lawful health information purposes, and you reside in a jurisdiction where this service is legally accessible.
terms.s3body: You are responsible for: maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials; all activities that occur under your account; providing accurate and up-to-date health information when prompted; using MamaVoice as a health information tool rather than a replacement for professional medical care; and complying with all applicable laws. Notify us immediately of any unauthorised account use at support{'@'}mamavoice.africa.
terms.s4body: You may use MamaVoice solely for lawful personal health information purposes. You agree not to: use the service to harass or harm others; attempt to gain unauthorised access to our systems; use automated bots or scrapers; reproduce or commercially exploit our content without permission; upload malicious code or viruses; impersonate other users or MamaVoice staff; or use the service in a way that could damage or overburden our infrastructure.
terms.s5body: IMPORTANT: MamaVoice uses artificial intelligence to provide maternal health information for educational purposes only. This does not constitute medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. AI-generated responses are based on general health guidelines and may not apply to your specific situation. Always consult a qualified doctor, midwife, or healthcare professional before making medical decisions. MamaVoice expressly disclaims any liability for health decisions made based solely on AI-generated content.
terms.s6body: ⚠️ MamaVoice is NOT an emergency medical service. If you are experiencing a medical emergency — including severe bleeding, sudden severe headache or blurred vision, signs of preeclampsia, difficulty breathing, loss of consciousness, reduced fetal movement, severe abdominal pain, or any life-threatening condition — call emergency services (112 in Nigeria) or go to your nearest hospital IMMEDIATELY. Do not rely on MamaVoice for emergency guidance.
terms.s7body: All content, features, and functionality of MamaVoice — including text, graphics, logos, software, and their arrangement — are the exclusive property of MamaVoice Technologies Ltd, protected by Nigerian and international intellectual property laws. You may not reproduce, distribute, modify, or commercially exploit any content from MamaVoice without our express written permission. You retain ownership of any personal health data you submit.
terms.s8body: By submitting content to MamaVoice — including questions, feedback, health data, and profile information — you grant MamaVoice a non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide licence to use, store, and process that content solely for providing and improving our service. We will never identify you personally in any publicly shared content. You represent that any content you submit does not infringe on third-party rights and is not illegal, harmful, or misleading.
terms.s9body: To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, MamaVoice Technologies Ltd shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, data, health outcomes, or other intangible losses resulting from your use of the service; any AI-generated health information; unauthorised access to your data; or any other matter relating to the service.
terms.s10body: MamaVoice strives to maintain high service availability. However, we do not guarantee that the service will be uninterrupted or error-free at all times. We may suspend access for maintenance, security updates, or circumstances beyond our control. We are not liable for loss or damage resulting from service interruptions. Always have an alternative contact with your healthcare provider for critical health needs.
terms.s11body: You may terminate your MamaVoice account at any time through the app settings or by contacting support{'@'}mamavoice.africa. Upon termination, your account data will be deleted within 30 days. MamaVoice reserves the right to suspend or terminate your account immediately without notice if you violate these Terms, engage in fraudulent or harmful behaviour, or pose a security risk to our systems or other users.
terms.s12body: These Terms of Service shall be governed by the laws of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. Any disputes shall first be attempted to be resolved through good-faith negotiation. If negotiation fails, disputes shall be submitted to binding arbitration in Lagos, Nigeria, in accordance with the Arbitration and Conciliation Act.
terms.s13body: For questions about these Terms: Email: legal{'@'}mamavoice.africa {'|'} Phone: +234 800 MAMA VOICE {'|'} MamaVoice Technologies Ltd, Lagos, Nigeria. Technical support: support{'@'}mamavoice.africa. Privacy concerns: privacy{'@'}mamavoice.africa. We aim to respond within 5 business days.

faq.sub: Everything you need to know before getting started with MamaVoice Companion.
faq.openSourceDesc: The MamaVoice web app is open source — developers and healthcare organisations can inspect, contribute to, and help improve the platform for every community.
faq.a0: MamaVoice Companion is a voice-first AI health platform designed specifically for African mothers. It provides trusted guidance on pregnancy, newborn care, nutrition, and vaccinations — in Yorùbá, Hausa, Igbo, English, and Nigerian Pidgin.
faq.q1: How does the voice assistant work?
faq.a1: Tap the microphone icon and speak naturally in your preferred language. MamaVoice uses advanced AI to understand your question and instantly responds with evidence-based maternal health guidance.
faq.a2: MamaVoice supports English, Yorùbá, Hausa, Igbo, and Nigerian Pidgin (Naija). We are continuously expanding language support to reach more communities across Africa.
faq.q3: Can I ask questions in Yorùbá, Hausa, Igbo, Pidgin, or English?
faq.a3: Yes! You can ask questions in any supported language. MamaVoice's AI understands natural speech in all five languages and responds in the same language you use.
faq.a4: Yes! The web app and core features are completely free. Premium features such as unlimited specialist consultations and advanced health tracking are available through affordable subscription plans.
faq.a5: Absolutely. Your health data is encrypted using industry-standard protocols, never sold to third parties, and stored securely in compliance with applicable data protection laws. Read our Privacy Policy for full details.
faq.q6: Does MamaVoice replace my doctor or midwife?
faq.a6: No. MamaVoice provides trusted health education and information, but is not a substitute for professional medical care. Always consult a qualified doctor, midwife, or healthcare professional for your specific needs.
faq.q7: Can I track my pregnancy and vaccinations?
faq.a7: Yes! MamaVoice includes a comprehensive health dashboard to track pregnancy progress, blood pressure, weight, baby kicks, and complete vaccination schedules for both mother and baby.
faq.q8: Does the platform work on mobile and desktop?
faq.a8: Yes! MamaVoice works seamlessly on any modern smartphone, tablet, or computer through your web browser. A dedicated mobile app for iOS and Android is also available.
faq.q9: Is the AI guidance medically reviewed?
faq.a9: Our AI is trained on evidence-based maternal health guidelines from the WHO, Federal Ministry of Health Nigeria, and leading medical journals. It provides reliable educational guidance but does not replace professional medical advice.
faq.q10: Can community health workers also use MamaVoice?
faq.a10: Absolutely! MamaVoice is designed to support community health workers, nurses, and midwives as well as mothers. It serves as a reliable reference during home visits, antenatal sessions, and community outreach programmes.
faq.q11: How do I switch between languages?
faq.a11: You can switch languages at any time using the language selector in the navigation bar or footer. Your preference is saved automatically and applied across the entire platform.

contact.metaDescription: Get in touch with MamaVoice. We're here to help with any questions about our maternal health platform.
contact.heroSub: Have a question, feedback, or need support? Our team is ready to help.
contact.formMessagePlaceholder: Tell us how we can help you…
contact.formSuccess: Thank you! Your message has been received. We'll get back to you within 24 hours.
contact.formError: Something went wrong. Please try again or email us directly at hello{'@'}mamavoice.africa.
contact.errNameMin: Name must be at least 2 characters.
contact.errEmailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
contact.errMessageMin: Message must be at least 10 characters.
contact.emergencyText: MamaVoice is not an emergency service. If you are experiencing a medical emergency, please call 112 immediately or go to your nearest hospital.
contact.faq0a: Yes! The MamaVoice web app and basic features are completely free. Premium features including unlimited specialist consultations and advanced health tracking are available through our affordable subscription plans.
contact.faq1a: MamaVoice supports English, Yorùbá, Hausa, Igbo, and Nigerian Pidgin. Our AI understands and responds in your preferred language, making maternal health guidance accessible to mothers across Nigeria and Africa.
contact.faq2q: How accurate is the AI health guidance?
contact.faq2a: Our AI is trained on evidence-based maternal health guidelines from WHO, FMOH Nigeria, and leading medical journals. While it provides reliable general guidance, it is not a substitute for professional medical advice. Always consult your doctor or midwife for personalised care.
contact.faq3q: Is my health data safe and private?
contact.faq3a: Absolutely. We take data privacy very seriously. Your health information is encrypted using industry-standard protocols, never sold to third parties, and only used to provide your personalised health guidance. Read our full Privacy Policy for details.
contact.faq4q: Can I use MamaVoice without internet?
contact.faq4a: The web app requires an internet connection. The mobile app (coming soon) will include offline support for essential features including saved health records, vaccination schedules, and pre-downloaded health guides.
contact.faq5q: How do I delete my account and data?
contact.faq5a: You can request account deletion at any time by going to Settings > Account > Delete Account in the app, or by emailing privacy{'@'}mamavoice.africa. All your personal data will be permanently deleted within 30 days of your request.
contact.communitySub: Connect with thousands of mothers in our WhatsApp support community for tips, encouragement, and shared experiences.
